summary: Tier 3: structural — strip leading title/byline + handle interleaved editorial footnotes
description:
Tier 3 (structural — lower volume, most care):

(a) Leading document title/byline repeated at chunk start, e.g.
    "The Life and Doctrines of Jacob Boehme, by Franz Hartmann, [translator]...".
    Strip via a per-source pre_strip or a chunker-level leading-title rule.
(b) Interleaved editorial / philological footnotes in the body: (sic) (~20),
    MS. / "in his MS" (~22), manuscript-collation notes. These are woven into
    real text — separate the footnote stream at PARSE time (downloader/chunker),
    NOT a blanket string strip (that would mangle sentences).

Re-chunk + re-embed affected. This is the hardest, lowest-impact child — may be
split further or partially deferred: if footnote-stream separation proves too
invasive, limit this child to the title/byline strip and note the deferral with
rationale on the ticket.

Done contract:
- Leading title/byline removed from affected chunks (verify on Boehme).
- Footnote handling implemented OR explicitly deferred with rationale on the ticket.
- Round-trip green; embeddings refreshed.

resolution.note: C4a done: added the per-page '<Title>, by <Translator>, [<year>], at sacred-texts.com' byline to BASELINE_PRE_STRIP (14 boehme chunks; validated 0 false-positives; guarded by test_no_scan_or_frontmatter_artifacts byline check). C4b DEFERRED with rationale: interleaved (sic)/MS. editorial marks (~46 chunks) are woven into real sentences — blanket-stripping mangles content, and parse-time footnote separation is disproportionate to the impact. Shipped with C3 in commit 934b10d.
